title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
author: Jasmine Garsd
summary: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal.

title: International Olympic Committee lifts Russia suspension ahead of 2028 LA Games
author: The Associated Press
summary: The International Olympic Committee advised sports bodies to end a three-year program vetting Russians for neutral status ahead of qualifying events for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

title: New lawsuit alleges U.S. shared asylum application details with Iran
author: Ximena Bustillo
summary: A lawsuit alleges the Trump administration is divulging details of Iranian asylum seekers to the government of Iran.

title: There's no treatment designed for the Ebola strain ravaging DRC. But now there's hope
author: Jonathan Lambert
summary: The strain of the virus that's responsible for the current outbreak has no specific treatments or preventive measures. Three new clinical trials could provide possible breakthroughs.

title: Woman accuses Graham Platner of sexual assault. And, NATO summit begins
author: Brittney Melton
summary: Graham Platner is facing calls to end his bid for U.S. Senate in Maine after a woman accused him of sexual assault. And, Trump made his dissatisfaction with NATO clear ahead of its summit this week.

title: Graham Platner faces calls to leave Maine Senate race after sexual assault allegation
author: Elena Moore
summary: Graham Platner is facing growing pressure to end his campaign for U.S. Senate in Maine after Politico reported an allegation of sexual assault. Platner has denied the claim.

title: Democratic strategist says transparency is Platner's 'only path forward'
author: Steve Inskeep
summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Democratic strategist Joel Payne about prominent Democrats on the national and state level urging Graham Platner to withdraw from the Maine Senate race.

title: Meet 'Project 2029' — and its war on the annoyance economy
author: Greg Rosalsky
summary: Remember Project 2025? Democrats are building their own governing blueprint, and one proposal takes aim at the "annoyance economy": robocalls, endless hold times, hidden fees and other everyday frustrations.

title: Trump's NATO pressure campaign continues as summit begins
author: Fatma Tanis
summary: President Trump's arrival in Ankara kicks off another potentially tense meeting for NATO.

title: As climate change damages streets and highways, the road ahead may be expensive
author: Scott Neuman
summary: Heat waves are becoming more common and intense as a result of climate change — and roads are suffering as a result. Are the nation's roads up to meeting the challenge of a warmer, wetter future?

title: Marine Le Pen to run for French presidency and appeal conviction in top court
summary: The hard-right National Rally leader has ended months of speculation, after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.

title: 2,000-year-old gold rings and human bones found by archaeologists
